recipeIngredient: ½ cup softened butter (or half shortening)
Beat for 2 more minutes; then blend in
1 tablespoon milk to ⅓ of the frosting. The remaining frosting stays plain.
recipeInstructions:
1. Follow the directions for mixing the cake batter. Pour into a greased, lightly floured 8 × 8 × 2‑inch pan.
2. Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 40–50 minutes. Cool completely.
3. Assemble by cutting the cooled nut loaf cake into four squares.
4. Frost two of the squares with white frosting and the other two with chocolate frosting.
5. Arrange the squares in a checkerboard pattern and sprinkle nuts over the top.
Best of class senior contest—1950 Pillsbury Cookoff. This is a simple cake that looks fancy; all you do is cut, frost, and arrange the pieces.
name: $1,000 Square Dance Nut Cake
